sessions.intent: Design the filesystem permission system: signed per-app grants on paths, with read/write/delete verbs each carrying sub-options. Path-shaped rewrite of IPLangFileSystem.

before.assumptions: Current FileAccessControl is runtime-only (in-memory FileAccessControl record, populated by yes/no/always prompt at exception time). No signature, no expiry, no audit. IPLangFileSystem inherits System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem (string-only). Cross-app reads (e.g. a messages app reading every app's system.sqlite) are not modelable today.
before.risk: Big surface rewrite — every action that touches the FS and every runtime internal that does the same. Signature work has to walk the full call graph. Goal-mapped FS provider is deliberately deferred.

actions.context: Added new section 'OBP Variant Design — When a Concept Has Multiple Configurable Modes'. Codifies four rules drawn from this design pass: singular folders, variant records with default-allow, owners-do-their-own-coverage, methods take whole domain objects. Also added a refinement to existing OBP guidance: verb-named methods are fine when they do real work — the GetX/IsX smell is about property-shaped questions, not verbs in general.

v1 plan for filesystem permission system. Spine + four topic deep-dives + five stage files + summary. Design settled in conversation with Ingi; three open conversation gates documented before stage 3.

after.status: plan written; awaiting Ingi to settle the three open gates (app-side cascade, Content sub-option on Read, process-only grant distinction) before stage 3 starts. Stage 1 can begin in parallel — pure types are independent.
after.health: good — design walked end-to-end (file.read from Messages to Email/system.sqlite), every concept has a named owner, no choreography across files.
after.notes: Branch newly created from origin/runtime2. good_to_know.md edit is real codification work, not session noise — captures lessons that should outlast this branch.

sessions.intent: Rewrite the plan to reflect a major design shift: permission generalizes per-subsystem (file/http/payment), Data.Request becomes a third terminal state, permission lives on the variable via Properties (lazy from store), and stages 1-3 bundle types + storage + full IPLangFileSystem refactor with permission baked in.

before.assumptions: Original plan v1 framed PermissionRequired as an Error type, kept the FS surface rewrite separate from the permission check site, and treated this branch as filesystem-only with no generalization story. Conversation with Ingi reframed all three.
before.risk: The shift is large enough that a partial update would leave the plan internally inconsistent. Stage files and topic docs need to move together with the spine.

actions.context: Rewrote spine + topic docs. Added runtime-flow.md (new). plan.md now leads with the pattern-not-base-class generalization, Data.Request third state, and the (c) bundle in stage 4. permission-design.md drops Check/Describe methods on the record — it's pure data with Covers. storage.md describes the two-store unification (in-memory + sqlite via system variables). filesystem-surface.md becomes the bundled IPLangFileSystem v2 design with permission baked in. open-questions.md retires most of the original list; ships five smaller still-open items.

Renumbered stages. Old stage-2 (storage), stage-3 (FS surface), stage-4 (PermissionRequired error) deleted; new stage-2 (Data.Request state), stage-3 (storage), stage-4 (IPLangFileSystem v2 bundle) written. Stage 1 (types) and stage 5 (e2e) updated in place. New ordering reflects the dependency chain: types → engine state → storage → FS rewrite → e2e.
Prepended 2026-05-19 entry summarizing the 10 design shifts and what previously-open questions resolved. Kept the 2026-05-14 entry below for history.

after.status: Plan rewritten initially to reflect 2026-05-19 design pass. Five stages defined, all pending. Stage 1 can start as-is (pure types, no dependencies). The big bundle is stage 4 — needs stages 2 and 3 first.
after.health: good — design walked end-to-end through file.read. Generalization pattern named for HTTP/Payment without prematurely codifying.
after.notes: Two follow-up branches implied by this design: an http-permission branch and a payment-permission branch, both consuming the Ask infrastructure landed in stage 2 of this branch.

sessions.intent: Walk through the plan with Ingi section by section; refine the design as it surfaces issues; propagate corrections across all plan files in one consolidated pass.

before.assumptions: Initial rewrite at 2026-05-19T13:00 had Data.Request as a third terminal state, FilePermission with AppId/Path/Match/Verb, storage as 'system variables backed by sqlite,' permission/@this as a manager class, and one-line code snippets in stage files.
before.risk: Walking the plan in interactive mode tends to surface design problems mid-step; without consolidating fixes, the plan grows inconsistent. Several corrections from Ingi struck OBP nerves: helper-soup, envelope-thinking, yes-man drift.

actions.context: Spine: section 1 framing rewritten to drop Data.Request as a third state in favour of Data.Fail with an Ask marker handled inside error.handle's built-in path. Section 3 runtime narrative rewritten — Path delegates to actor.Permission (not App.Permissions); engine owns retry loop; ask handler is stateless. Cross-cutting decisions updated for 2-column storage rule, actor-as-record-field, JSON-filter scoping. Stage 2 renamed/relinked to stage-2-ask-routing.md.

Topic docs rewritten. runtime-flow.md: ask handling inside error.handle, two-layer lookup (Properties cache → actor.Permission), engine-side retry. permission-design.md: FilePermission gains Actor field; Path.CheckPermission takes verb kind only; no ReadOnly/WriteOnly factories; the manager-class concept dissolved into actor.Permission. storage.md: rewritten around App.SettingsStore + 2-column rule + actor-as-JSON-filter; no separate persistence layer; one permission table not three. filesystem-surface.md: code blocks stripped (architect rule); permission check delegated to Path via path.CheckPermission. open-questions.md: throw-vs-return resolved as return; ask shape resolved as Data.Fail+Ask.

Stage files updated consistently. Stage 1 references Ask marker work as stage 2's concern. Stage 2 renamed (data-request-state → ask-routing), reframed around error.handle's built-in path. Stage 3 reframed around Actor.@this.Permission (per-actor view) + path.CheckPermission on Path class; storage is App.SettingsStore 'permission' table with 2-column rule; per-actor scoping via JSON filter on Actor field. Stage 4 references path.CheckPermission(Verb.X) interface. Stage 5 acceptance checks the permission table directly.

New file. Proposes a 5th OBP smell to good_to_know.md: 'Helper that takes a domain object and returns a derived answer.' Includes worked example contrasting helper-soup (Resolve(path), CheckPermission(absolute, verb)) with self-owning methods (path.Absolute, path.CheckPermission(verb)).

Prepended a new 2026-05-19 (continued) entry summarizing the refinements: storage on App.SettingsStore directly; 2-column rule; actor-on-record with JSON-filter scoping; path.CheckPermission(verb kind) signature; verb sub-options stay in C#; four-level delegation; error.handle is the umbrella; CLAUDE.md proposal filed.

after.status: Plan internally consistent end-to-end after the walkthrough. All stage files, topic docs, summary, and the CLAUDE.md proposal reflect the same model. Stage 1 can begin; stage 2 has a clear acceptance contract; stage 3 has a concrete shape for actor.Permission + Path.CheckPermission; stage 4 references both.
after.health: good — design walked end-to-end with Ingi, multiple OBP corrections caught and incorporated. The plan now reads as one coherent design rather than a sequence of accumulated decisions.
after.notes: Major patterns codified during the session: (1) FilePermission carries Actor field; (2) Path owns CheckPermission; (3) actor.Permission unifies in-memory + persisted; (4) error.handle owns ask routing internally; (5) 2-column storage rule. The helper-soup OBP smell was the most generalizable lesson — filed as a CLAUDE.md proposal.

sessions.intent: Translate architect's 5-stage filesystem-permission design into a test contract — C# TUnit stubs + PLang test goals — that the coder makes pass.

before.assumptions: Architect plan is final after Ingi's review pass (33 comments resolved). 5 stages: types, snapshot/resume, Path.Authorize, storage, IPLangFileSystem v2, end-to-end Messages. Existing CallbackTests/ folder will be rewritten around Snapshot instead of per-callback classes.
before.risk: Volume is large (~100 tests). Stage 4 parametrization needs the coder to confirm the [Arguments] shape. 'No App.Run / App.RunAction' tests are static-survey style — that pattern may not be established yet.

actions.context: 10-batch breakdown across the 5 stages. Approved up front by user — no per-batch walk-through needed.
Stage 1 — Verb sub-record cover matrix + Permission.Covers with closed Match dispatch and JSON round-trip.
Stage 2a — markers, Snapshot field+helper, ShouldExit, RunFrom, RunAsync, output.ask routing, Snapshot.Resume. One concern per file.
Stage 2a — PLang integration. Mid-goal stateful + cross-goal stateless suspend/resume (the canonical Start→AskAQuestion case).
Stage 2b — Path.Authorize answer cases (a/y/n/garbage), stateless bubble, constructed Permission shape, PermissionDenied; plus stateful and stateless e2e.
Stage 3 — Find/Add/Revoke; per-actor JSON-filter scoping; in-memory + sqlite unification; signature caching; idempotent Add.

Stage 4 — parametrized per-method flow (in-root, out-of-root stateful, out-of-root stateless); shape contracts (no IFileSystem inheritance, no ValidatePath, no FileAccessControl); Move/Copy bundled consent. Parametrization shape (MethodDataSource) will need a coder review.

Stage 5 — six-step integration test broken into six independent PLang test goals: no-grant suspend → grant 'a' stores → immediate reread skips → restart still skips → revoke reprompts → narrowed grant rejects wider request.

after.status: Test contract drafted. ~70 C# test stubs + 11 PLang test goal stubs. All bodies are Assert.Fail / throw "not implemented". Ready for coder.
after.notes: Open follow-ups (logged in plan.md): (1) some Batch 6 'stateful e2e' tests may actually need real Stage 3 storage rather than mocks — coder to decide; (2) TUnit MethodDataSource shape in Stage 4 parametrization needs a coder review; (3) static-survey 'no App.Run / App.RunAction' tests may need a new helper.
